essay_text:
Unfinished concrete houses slumped wearily in the amber dirt as I made my way through the village of Aiyepe,
Ogun State, Nigeria whose residents’ smiles were warm with welcome. It was in this small town that my
grandfather went to school, later winning a scholarship to university in Ireland. As one of nine children in an area
riddled with poverty, his opportunity to study History at undergraduate level was like a dream. I intended to visit the
school where my grandfather was educated to see his impact on the community and capture the results on film.
I ambled down the dirt road, clutching my camera tightly in my hand, eager to catch the various aspects of African
school-life. It was around this point that my interest in film-making was developing: a YouTube page was
beginning to fill with homemade short films which my relatives told me showed glimpses of talent. I didn’t know
what to expect from Aiyepe community school; my grandfather had often spoken of how lucky I was to have
classrooms with air-conditioning and every seat in the class occupied by a student. When I reached the front of the
school, I was anticipating empty fields with one or two children in sight. However, I was greeted by a wall of
smiling teachers and students dressed smartly in deep blue-and-white uniforms. They had been anticipating my
arrival.
I was quickly swept away by the students who insisted I take a tour of their thriving academy. Their classrooms
were simple with nothing more than a few chairs, desks and a blackboard. However, their desire to learn was
comprehensive. One student showed me their school football pitch, declaring their dream to become a footballer
akin to Messi.
I began to video the children going about their activities from a distance. I expected to witness some classic
Yoruba chants but soon realized that the way the children played in this Nigerian school was no different from that
of my own in London. The poverty and lack of drive my grandfather used to describe had transformed into a
bustling energy and will to succeed.
Standing by the football pitches, a teacher beckoned me over, with a story to share. She led me to a building
constructed out of dark, polished wood, glistening under the heat of the sun. Engraved into the front panel in gold
letters was the name of my grandfather.
Johnson Pase Memorial Hall.
Inside, students drew pictures and pupils’ work hung from the walls. The building was being used as a place to
foster creativity - something which until recently was not considered important within Nigerian education. The
teacher told me that the building, in addition to funds provided by my grandfather, was the reason the school and
CA Akinluyi, Christopher CEEB: 724324 Fall 2020
9 FYRD CAID: 23031142
the village were thriving. My desire to inspire people to creativity was immediate. I realized that effecting change
through creativity required my total commitment to the path of film-making. By making films and sharing them with
the world, I feel that not only would people be inspired to create their own art, but they would also be inspired to
positively change their environment through creative means.
I left the school later that afternoon and watched the barrage of deep blue uniforms shrink into distant, swirling
waves. My goodbye to the students was hopeful as their passion for their futures had resonated deep within me. I
imagined my grandfather at my age: a young man surrounded by poverty, with a desire to mend the inequality of
opportunity around him. Compared to him, I feel I have achieved very little with very much presented to me: I
haven’t achieved anything of substance yet with my film-making. I cannot squander everything he worked for by
being content with his legacy as my own. If my grandfather could achieve so much having started out with so little,
where is the limit of my change?
prompt: Discuss an accomplishment, event, or realization that sparked a period of personal growth and a new understanding of yourself or others.
persona_prompt: You are a reflective and observant writer who crafts narratives around personal experiences, using vivid sensory details to draw the reader into a scene. You often employ contrast between expectation and reality, or past and present, to highlight deeper insights. Integrate moments of internal monologue and epiphany, culminating in a clear statement of purpose or a profound personal commitment, all while maintaining a sophisticated yet accessible vocabulary.
sentence_style: Varied sentence length, often combining longer, descriptive complex sentences with occasional short, impactful statements. The rhythm is fluid and narrative-driven, with clear articulation of observations and internal thoughts.
vocabulary_level: Sophisticated — characterized by precise, evocative word choices (e.g., "slumped wearily," "riddled," "ambled," "thriving academy," "barrage," "squander") that elevate the description without being overly academic.
structural_patterns: Narrative journey structure, beginning with a setting and evolving through a sequence of observations and interactions. Employs strong contrast between initial expectations and the reality encountered. Integrates a personal connection (grandfather's legacy) as a catalyst for self-reflection and a culminating statement of personal commitment.
distinctive_moves: Evocative sensory descriptions that immerse the reader in the setting (e.g., 'amber dirt,' 'deep blue-and-white uniforms,' 'dark, polished wood, glistening').
Juxtaposition of expectation versus reality to create a sense of discovery and deeper understanding.
Seamless integration of internal monologue and personal reflection within the narrative flow.
Uses a significant discovery (e.g., the memorial hall) as a symbolic anchor for a shift in perspective or an epiphany.
Concludes with a strong, self-challenging rhetorical question and a clear articulation of future purpose, linking personal ambition to broader societal impact.
themes_and_preoccupations: The enduring power of legacy and intergenerational influence.
The transformative role of education and creativity in challenging poverty and fostering progress.
The importance of challenging preconceived notions and embracing evolving realities.
Personal responsibility to leverage privilege and opportunity for positive societal change.
self_presentation: Observational, reflective, somewhat vulnerable (especially when comparing self to grandfather), earnest, and driven. Presents as a thoughtful individual grappling with purpose and the weight of inherited opportunity.
cultural_or_contextual_markers: Nigerian village life and educational context, contrast between Western and African experiences, and a family history rooted in overcoming socioeconomic challenges through education.
avoid_in_imitation: Overly casual or simplistic language; maintain a sophisticated yet natural tone.
Purely descriptive narrative without internal reflection, personal growth, or a clear arc of realization.
Statements of purpose that feel ungrounded or lack a narrative build-up from personal experience.
sample_sentence: The old atlas, worn smooth by generations of curious hands, lay open to a map of continents, each border a whisper of journeys yet to unfold, prompting a quiet stir of resolve within me.
